rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference component ports
Place implementation-level bindings in component implementation annexes after subcomponents and connections
Use only visible identifiers from the current scope or the AADL model for AGREE expressions
generic_rules: Maintain the authoritative source of component structure from the input AADL model
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL property associations as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
Align helper declaration types with the expression types to avoid type mismatches
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks for AGREE contracts
For range comparisons, use the pattern `eq range_value : bool = x >= min_value and x <= max_value;`
For output dependency contracts, relate visible outputs to visible inputs in component type annexes
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
Avoid range comparisons or 0/1 equality over Boolean, enumeration-like, or opaque data ports unless the port is an AGREE bool in scope
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
